title.en: Vasectomy Cost Without Insurance in 2026 — National Pricing Guide | CoveredUSA
title.es: Costo de Vasectomia sin Seguro en 2026 — Guia Nacional de Precios | CoveredUSA

description.en: Vasectomy cost in 2026: $300 to $1,500 without insurance. Compare office, ASC, and hospital prices. Medicare does not cover elective vasectomy. ACA mandate does not apply to men.
description.es: Costo de vasectomia en 2026: $300 a $1,500 sin seguro. Compare precios en consultorio, centro de cirugia ambulatoria y hospital. Medicare no cubre vasectomia electiva.

h1.en: How Much Does a Vasectomy Cost Without Insurance in 2026?
h1.es: ¿Cuánto cuesta una vasectomia sin seguro en 2026?

subhero.en: Without insurance, a vasectomy typically costs $300 to $1,500 at a urologist's office or clinic, and $1,500 to $3,500 at a hospital outpatient department. The national median for an office-based vasectomy is around $750. Site of service is the biggest cost driver, and in-office procedures are nearly always cheaper than hospital-based ones.
subhero.es: Sin seguro, una vasectomia tipicamente cuesta de $300 a $1,500 en el consultorio de un urologo o clinica, y de $1,500 a $3,500 en un departamento ambulatorio de hospital. La mediana nacional para una vasectomia en consultorio es de alrededor de $750. El sitio de servicio es el mayor determinante del costo.

quickAnswer.en: A vasectomy costs $300 to $1,500 at a urologist's office without insurance in 2026, with a national median near $750. Ambulatory surgery centers charge $800 to $1,800, and hospital outpatient departments charge $1,500 to $3,500 for the same procedure. Medicare does not cover elective vasectomy for contraception. The ACA contraceptive mandate does not apply to vasectomy under federal law, though 9 states require commercial plans to cover it.
quickAnswer.es: Una vasectomia cuesta de $300 a $1,500 en el consultorio de un urologo sin seguro en 2026, con una mediana nacional cercana a $750. Los centros de cirugia ambulatoria cobran de $800 a $1,800, y los departamentos ambulatorios de hospital cobran de $1,500 a $3,500 por el mismo procedimiento. Medicare no cubre la vasectomia electiva para anticoncepcion.

introParagraphs.en: A vasectomy is one of the most cost-effective forms of permanent contraception available. The procedure takes 15 to 30 minutes, is performed under local anesthesia, and in most cases requires no hospital stay. Despite its simplicity, the price without insurance varies widely, from under $350 at a Planned Parenthood clinic to over $3,000 at a hospital outpatient department.
introParagraphs.es: Una vasectomia es una de las formas mas rentables de anticoncepcion permanente disponible. El procedimiento toma de 15 a 30 minutos, se realiza bajo anestesia local y en la mayoria de los casos no requiere hospitalizacion. A pesar de su sencillez, el precio sin seguro varia ampliamente, desde menos de $350 en una clinica de Planned Parenthood hasta mas de $3,000 en un departamento ambulatorio de hospital.

introParagraphs.en: The single biggest factor driving that price gap is site of service. Research published in a 2025 peer-reviewed study found that in-office vasectomies averaged $707, compared to $1,851 at ambulatory surgery centers (ASC), and hospital outpatient settings were consistently the most expensive of the three. The procedure itself is identical. Only the facility fee changes. Patients who have met their annual [deductible](/en/glossary/deductible) may find timing an elective procedure before year-end significantly reduces their cost.
introParagraphs.es: El factor mas importante que genera esa brecha de precios es el sitio de servicio. Una investigacion publicada en un estudio revisado por pares en 2025 encontro que las vasectomias en consultorio promediaron $707, en comparacion con $1,851 en centros de cirugia ambulatoria (ASC), y los entornos ambulatorios de hospital eran consistentemente los mas caros. El procedimiento en si es identico. Solo cambia la tarifa de instalacion.

introParagraphs.en: This guide covers what a vasectomy costs without insurance in 2026 by setting, why Medicare does not cover it, what the ACA does and does not require, and how to avoid the most common billing errors on a vasectomy bill. Low-income patients can check [Medicaid income limits](/en/medicaid-income-limits) — some state Medicaid programs cover vasectomies as a family planning benefit at $0 cost.
introParagraphs.es: Esta guia cubre lo que cuesta una vasectomia sin seguro en 2026 segun el entorno, por que Medicare no la cubre, que exige y que no exige la ACA, y como evitar los errores de facturacion mas comunes en una factura de vasectomia.

siteName.en: Planned Parenthood or reproductive health clinic
siteName.es: Planned Parenthood o clinica de salud reproductiva

explanationParagraphs.en: Hospital outpatient departments add facility fees that cover overhead, nursing, and operating room time, costs that a urologist's office does not pass on because the physician absorbs them into the professional fee. A 2025 peer-reviewed analysis confirmed that facility fees are the primary driver of the wide price range for vasectomy, accounting for as much as 84% of cost variability across settings.
explanationParagraphs.es: Los departamentos ambulatorios de hospital agregan cargos de instalacion que cubren gastos generales, enfermeria y tiempo de quirofano, costos que el consultorio de un urologo no traslada porque el medico los absorbe en la tarifa profesional. Un analisis revisado por pares de 2025 confirmo que los cargos de instalacion son el principal impulsor del amplio rango de precios de la vasectomia, representando hasta el 84% de la variabilidad de costos entre entornos.

explanationParagraphs.en: Ambulatory surgery centers sit in the middle: they have more overhead than a doctor's office but less than a full hospital. ASC-based vasectomies average around $1,851, versus $707 for in-office procedures, according to research published in Translational Andrology and Urology (2025). Most urologists can perform a standard vasectomy safely in the office under local anesthesia, with no medical reason to use an ASC or hospital unless sedation is requested.
explanationParagraphs.es: Los centros de cirugia ambulatoria se situan en el medio: tienen mas gastos generales que un consultorio medico pero menos que un hospital completo. Las vasectomias en ASC promedian alrededor de $1,851, frente a $707 para procedimientos en consultorio, segun investigacion publicada en Translational Andrology and Urology (2025). La mayoria de los urologos pueden realizar una vasectomia estandar de forma segura en el consultorio bajo anestesia local, sin razon medica para usar un ASC u hospital a menos que se solicite sedacion.

explanationParagraphs.en: Planned Parenthood and community reproductive health clinics often offer the lowest cash prices, sometimes sliding-scale or free for lower-income patients. Planned Parenthood publicly states that vasectomies can cost between $0 and $1,000 including follow-up visits. Always call ahead to confirm availability and pricing at your nearest location.
explanationParagraphs.es: Planned Parenthood y las clinicas comunitarias de salud reproductiva suelen ofrecer los precios al contado mas bajos, a veces en escala movil o gratis para pacientes de menores ingresos. Planned Parenthood declara publicamente que las vasectomias pueden costar entre $0 y $1,000 incluidas las visitas de seguimiento. Siempre llame con anticipacion para confirmar disponibilidad y precios en su ubicacion mas cercana.

tableFootnote.en: Ranges reflect 2025-2026 national data from FAIR Health, ClearHealthCosts, and peer-reviewed cost modeling. Sliding-scale pricing at clinics can reduce out-of-pocket cost to $0 based on income.
tableFootnote.es: Los rangos reflejan datos nacionales de 2025-2026 de FAIR Health, ClearHealthCosts y modelos de costos revisados por pares. Los precios en escala movil en clinicas pueden reducir el costo de bolsillo a $0 segun los ingresos.

siteOfService.tableSource: Translational Andrology and Urology (2025), ClearHealthCosts, Planned Parenthood, FAIR Health Consumer

heading.es: Costo de Vasectomia por Tecnica en 2026

intro.en: Most urologists now offer no-scalpel vasectomy as their standard technique. Both approaches use the same billing code (CPT 55250) and cost about the same. The meaningful price difference comes from setting, not technique. Some providers also offer open-ended vasectomy, which leaves one end of the vas deferens unsealed to reduce pressure-related discomfort.
intro.es: La mayoria de los urologos ofrecen ahora la vasectomia sin bisturi como tecnica estandar. Ambos enfoques usan el mismo codigo de facturacion (CPT 55250) y cuestan aproximadamente lo mismo. La diferencia de precio significativa proviene del entorno, no de la tecnica. Algunos proveedores tambien ofrecen vasectomia de extremo abierto, que deja un extremo del conducto deferente sin sellar para reducir las molestias relacionadas con la presion.

en: Two small incisions; same CPT 55250 code
es: Dos pequenas incisiones; mismo codigo CPT 55250

en: Single puncture; less bleeding; same billing code
es: Puncion unica; menos sangrado; mismo codigo de facturacion

en: May use CPT 55450 at some practices; confirm with provider
es: Algunas practicas usan CPT 55450; confirme con el proveedor

footnote.en: CPT 55250 covers conventional and standard no-scalpel vasectomy. CPT 55450 (ligation of vas deferens, percutaneous) may be used for needle-free approaches at some practices. Both are professional fees only; facility fees are added when performed outside the physician's own office.
footnote.es: CPT 55250 cubre vasectomia convencional y sin bisturi estandar. CPT 55450 (ligadura del conducto deferente, percutanea) puede usarse para enfoques sin aguja en algunas practicas. Ambas son solo honorarios profesionales; se agregan cargos de instalacion cuando se realiza fuera del consultorio propio del medico.

variants.source: AAPC Coding Guidelines, CMS Physician Fee Schedule 2026

paragraphs.en: Original Medicare (Parts A and B) does not cover vasectomy performed for elective contraceptive sterilization. CMS National Coverage Determination 230.3 states that elective sterilization procedures, including vasectomy, are excluded from Medicare coverage when performed solely for contraception. If a vasectomy is medically necessary for a documented condition other than contraception (for example, to treat a vas deferens obstruction, or as part of cancer-related surgery), a physician may bill Medicare using a diagnosis code that supports medical necessity, and Medicare would consider it on a case-by-case basis. However, this is rare.
paragraphs.es: Medicare Original (Partes A y B) no cubre la vasectomia realizada para esterilizacion anticonceptiva electiva. La Determinacion de Cobertura Nacional 230.3 de CMS establece que los procedimientos de esterilizacion electiva, incluida la vasectomia, estan excluidos de la cobertura de Medicare cuando se realizan unicamente para anticoncepcion. Si una vasectomia es medicamente necesaria por una condicion documentada distinta a la anticoncepcion, Medicare puede considerarla caso por caso. Sin embargo, esto es poco comun.

paragraphs.en: Medicare Advantage plans set their own supplemental benefits. Some plans cover vasectomy as an additional benefit, though this varies significantly by plan and region. If you have Medicare Advantage, review your Summary of Benefits or call your plan directly to ask whether vasectomy is covered and what cost-sharing applies. If your plan does cover it, you would still pay any applicable copay or coinsurance after meeting your plan deductible.
paragraphs.es: Los planes Medicare Advantage establecen sus propios beneficios suplementarios. Algunos planes cubren la vasectomia como un beneficio adicional, aunque esto varia significativamente segun el plan y la region. Si tiene Medicare Advantage, revise su Resumen de Beneficios o llame directamente a su plan para preguntar si la vasectomia esta cubierta y que participacion en el costo aplica.

items.en: Site of service: office ($300-$1,200), ASC ($800-$1,800), hospital outpatient ($1,500-$3,500). The largest cost factor.
items.es: Sitio de servicio: consultorio ($300-$1,200), ASC ($800-$1,800), hospital ambulatorio ($1,500-$3,500). El mayor factor de costo.

items.en: Geographic region: urban markets (New York, California) tend to be higher; Midwest and Southern states are often lower.
items.es: Region geografica: los mercados urbanos (Nueva York, California) tienden a ser mas altos; los estados del Medio Oeste y del Sur suelen ser mas bajos.

items.en: Whether sedation or anesthesia is used (adds facility and anesthesia fees if performed at an ASC or hospital).
items.es: Si se usa sedacion o anestesia (agrega cargos de instalacion y anestesia si se realiza en un ASC u hospital).

items.en: Follow-up semen analysis: typically $75 per test, with two tests usually required to confirm sterility.
items.es: Analisis de semen de seguimiento: tipicamente $75 por prueba, con dos pruebas generalmente requeridas para confirmar esterilidad.

items.en: Insurance coverage: some commercial plans cover vasectomy. Nine states (California, Illinois, Maryland, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, Vermont, Washington) require state-regulated plans to cover vasectomy at no cost.
items.es: Cobertura de seguro: algunos planes comerciales cubren la vasectomia. Nueve estados requieren que los planes regulados por el estado cubran la vasectomia sin costo.

items.en: Income-based sliding scale: Planned Parenthood and federally qualified health centers (FQHCs) may reduce or waive the fee based on income.
items.es: Escala movil basada en ingresos: Planned Parenthood y los centros de salud federalmente calificados (FQHC) pueden reducir o eximir la tarifa segun los ingresos.

items.en: Urologist experience and practice type: high-volume vasectomy specialists often charge less than general urologists because of practice efficiency.
items.es: Experiencia del urologo y tipo de practica: los especialistas en vasectomia de alto volumen a menudo cobran menos que los urologos generales debido a la eficiencia de la practica.

intro.en: Vasectomy billing is relatively straightforward, but these errors appear regularly on claims and EOBs. Review your bill before paying:
intro.es: La facturacion de vasectomia es relativamente sencilla, pero estos errores aparecen regularmente en reclamaciones y EOBs. Revise su factura antes de pagar:

items.en: Facility fee charged when the procedure was performed in the physician's own office (in-office professional fees should not include a separate facility component).
items.es: Cargo de instalacion cobrado cuando el procedimiento se realizo en el consultorio propio del medico (los honorarios profesionales en consultorio no deben incluir un componente de instalacion separado).

items.en: Anesthesia billed when only local anesthesia was administered (local anesthesia is included in the surgical fee and should not be billed separately).
items.es: Anestesia facturada cuando solo se administro anestesia local (la anestesia local esta incluida en la tarifa quirurgica y no debe facturarse por separado).

items.en: Billed as a bilateral procedure when a vasectomy is inherently bilateral (both vas deferens are cut in a single procedure; some billers incorrectly add modifier -50 and double the fee).
items.es: Facturado como procedimiento bilateral cuando una vasectomia es inherentemente bilateral (ambos conductos deferentes se cortan en un solo procedimiento; algunos facturadores agregan incorrectamente el modificador -50 y duplican la tarifa).

items.en: Semen analysis post-procedure billed at a hospital laboratory rate when it was performed at a lower-cost reference lab.
items.es: Analisis de semen post-procedimiento facturado a la tarifa de un laboratorio hospitalario cuando se realizo en un laboratorio de referencia de menor costo.

items.en: Consultation visit coded at a higher E&M level than the complexity of the pre-vasectomy counseling visit warranted.
items.es: Visita de consulta codificada en un nivel E&M superior al que ameritaba la complejidad de la visita de consejeria previa a la vasectomia.

items.en: Scrotal support, ice pack, or minor supplies billed as separate durable medical equipment (DME) items when they should be bundled into the procedure fee.
items.es: Soporte escrotal, bolsa de hielo u otros suministros menores facturados como articulos de equipo medico duradero (DME) separados cuando deberian incluirse en la tarifa del procedimiento.

en.question: How much does a vasectomy cost without insurance in 2026?
en.answer: Without insurance, a vasectomy costs $300 to $1,200 at a urologist's office, $800 to $1,800 at an ambulatory surgery center, and $1,500 to $3,500 at a hospital outpatient department. The national median for an office-based procedure is around $750. Planned Parenthood charges $0 to $1,000 on a sliding scale based on income.

en.question: Does Medicare cover vasectomy?
en.answer: No. Original Medicare does not cover vasectomy performed for elective contraceptive sterilization. CMS National Coverage Determination 230.3 excludes elective sterilization procedures from Medicare coverage. Some Medicare Advantage plans cover vasectomy as a supplemental benefit, but coverage varies by plan. Call your Medicare Advantage plan directly to ask.

en.question: Is vasectomy covered by the ACA contraceptive mandate?
en.answer: No. The ACA preventive services mandate for contraception applies to services with respect to women under HRSA guidelines. Vasectomy is not included in the federal mandate. Nine states, including California, New York, and Oregon, require state-regulated commercial plans to cover vasectomy at no cost. This state-level requirement does not apply to self-insured employer plans, which cover most working Americans.

en.question: What is the cheapest way to get a vasectomy?
en.answer: The most affordable options are Planned Parenthood (sliding-scale pricing from $0 to $1,000) and high-volume vasectomy specialty clinics. An office-based vasectomy under local anesthesia at a urologist or reproductive health clinic is significantly cheaper than one done at an ASC or hospital. Avoiding sedation also eliminates the anesthesia and facility fees that drive ASC costs to $800-$1,800.

en.question: Why is a vasectomy at a hospital so much more expensive?
en.answer: Hospitals bill a facility fee that covers overhead, operating room time, nursing, and administrative costs. These fees are not present when the same physician performs the procedure in their own office. A 2025 peer-reviewed study found facility fees account for up to 84% of the cost variability in vasectomy pricing, with in-office procedures averaging $707 versus $1,851 at ambulatory surgery centers.

en.question: Does my health insurance cover vasectomy?
en.answer: It depends on your state and plan. Nine states require state-regulated plans to cover vasectomy at no cost. Outside those states, coverage varies widely. Some plans cover it with a copay or coinsurance; others exclude it entirely. Call your insurer and ask specifically: 'Is CPT code 55250 covered under my plan, and what is my cost-sharing?' Get the answer in writing or note the representative's name and date.

en.question: What does a vasectomy include in the total cost?
en.answer: The base fee typically covers the procedure itself and the pre-procedure office visit or consultation. Follow-up semen analyses are usually billed separately, around $75 per test, and two tests are typically required at 8 and 12 weeks post-procedure to confirm sterility. If you need sedation, an anesthesia fee and facility fee will be added on top of the surgeon's fee.

en.question: Is no-scalpel vasectomy more expensive than conventional vasectomy?
en.answer: Not meaningfully. Both conventional and no-scalpel vasectomy use CPT code 55250 and are billed at the same rate. The price difference between the two techniques is minimal at the same facility. The larger cost driver is always the setting: the same no-scalpel vasectomy costs far more at a hospital than in a urologist's office.

es.question: ¿Cuánto cuesta una vasectomia sin seguro en 2026?
es.answer: Sin seguro, una vasectomia cuesta de $300 a $1,200 en el consultorio de un urologo, de $800 a $1,800 en un centro de cirugia ambulatoria y de $1,500 a $3,500 en un departamento ambulatorio de hospital. La mediana nacional para un procedimiento en consultorio es de alrededor de $750. Planned Parenthood cobra de $0 a $1,000 en escala movil segun los ingresos.

es.question: ¿Medicare cubre la vasectomia?
es.answer: No. Medicare Original no cubre la vasectomia realizada para esterilizacion anticonceptiva electiva. La Determinacion de Cobertura Nacional 230.3 de CMS excluye los procedimientos de esterilizacion electiva de la cobertura de Medicare. Algunos planes Medicare Advantage cubren la vasectomia como beneficio suplementario, pero la cobertura varia segun el plan. Llame directamente a su plan Medicare Advantage para preguntar.

es.question: ¿La vasectomia esta cubierta por el mandato de anticoncepcion de la ACA?
es.answer: No. El mandato de servicios preventivos de la ACA para anticoncepcion se aplica a servicios para mujeres segun las directrices de HRSA. La vasectomia no esta incluida en el mandato federal. Nueve estados, incluidos California, Nueva York y Oregon, requieren que los planes comerciales regulados por el estado cubran la vasectomia sin costo. Este requisito estatal no aplica a los planes de empleadores autoasegurados.

es.question: ¿Cual es la forma mas economica de obtener una vasectomia?
es.answer: Las opciones mas asequibles son Planned Parenthood (precios en escala movil de $0 a $1,000) y las clinicas especializadas en vasectomia de alto volumen. Una vasectomia en consultorio bajo anestesia local es significativamente mas barata que una realizada en un ASC o hospital. Evitar la sedacion tambien elimina los honorarios de anestesia y de instalacion.

es.question: ¿Por que una vasectomia en un hospital es mucho mas cara?
es.answer: Los hospitales cobran un cargo de instalacion que cubre gastos generales, tiempo de quirofano, enfermeria y costos administrativos. Estos cargos no existen cuando el mismo medico realiza el procedimiento en su propio consultorio. Un estudio revisado por pares de 2025 encontro que los cargos de instalacion representan hasta el 84% de la variabilidad de costos, con procedimientos en consultorio promediando $707 frente a $1,851 en centros de cirugia ambulatoria.

es.question: ¿Mi seguro medico cubre la vasectomia?
es.answer: Depende de su estado y plan. Nueve estados requieren que los planes regulados por el estado cubran la vasectomia sin costo. Fuera de esos estados, la cobertura varia ampliamente. Llame a su aseguradora y pregunte especificamente: '¿El codigo CPT 55250 esta cubierto en mi plan y cual es mi participacion en el costo?'

es.question: ¿Que incluye el costo total de una vasectomia?
es.answer: La tarifa base generalmente cubre el procedimiento en si y la visita o consulta previa al procedimiento. Los analisis de semen de seguimiento generalmente se facturan por separado, alrededor de $75 por prueba, y se requieren dos pruebas tipicamente a las 8 y 12 semanas para confirmar la esterilidad. Si necesita sedacion, se agregaran honorarios de anestesia y de instalacion.

es.question: ¿La vasectomia sin bisturi es mas cara que la convencional?
es.answer: No significativamente. Tanto la vasectomia convencional como la sin bisturi usan el codigo CPT 55250 y se facturan a la misma tarifa. El mayor determinante del costo siempre es el entorno: la misma vasectomia sin bisturi cuesta mucho mas en un hospital que en el consultorio de un urologo.
